etymology.description: 啤(啤) (pí, "beer") + 酒(酒) (jiǔ, "alcoholic drink") = alcoholic drink made of malt and hops, paralleling to beer in the Western sense
etymology.interpretation: 啤(啤) (pí, "beer") was borrowed from English or European languages as a phonetic loan for 'beer,' emphasizing the foreign introduction of this type of alcoholic beverage. 酒(酒) (jiǔ, "alcoholic drink") is a general term for any fermented alcoholic beverage in Chinese, such as wine and spirits. Together, 啤酒(啤酒) (píjiǔ, "beer") combines the foreign concept of beer with the traditional Chinese term for alcohol, linking them as a specific type of alcoholic beverage.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢喝啤酒(我喜歡喝啤酒)。
我们去酒吧喝了几杯啤酒(我們去酒吧喝了幾杯啤酒)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen qù jiǔbā hē le jǐ bēi píjiǔ.
examples.english: We went to the bar and drank a few glasses of beer.

examples.chinese: 他在冰箱里放了一罐啤酒(他在冰箱裡放了一罐啤酒)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài bīngxiāng lǐ fàng le yī guàn píjiǔ.
examples.english: He put a can of beer in the fridge.
